---
# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-082 — Wave 2 Integration Proof — Exemplar Runtime Validation Gate
# Generated by build-pipeline / lead-architect + backend-service-builder
# Generated at: 2026-05-12T00:07:44Z

prd: PRD-082
prd_title: "Wave 2 Integration Proof — Exemplar Runtime Validation Gate"
service: PlayerFinancialService
mvp_phase: 2

fib_h: docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/wave-2/w-2-integration-proof/FIB-W2-INTEGRATION-PROOF.md
fib_s: docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/wave-2/w-2-integration-proof/fib-s-integration-proof.json

scope_amendment:
  type: validation-gate
  date: "2026-05-12"
  allowed_change_type: validation_and_test_harness_only
  business_behavior_change: false
  producer_expansion: false
  projection_implementation: false
  operator_visible_surface_change: false

overengineering_guardrail: docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/OVERENGINEERING_GUARDRAIL_OUTBOX_WAVE2.md

claim_reclaim_finding: >
  rpc_claim_outbox_batch (migration 20260511134531) uses WHERE processed_at IS NULL with
  FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED. No persistent claim-lock state — lock releases when function
  transaction commits; processed_at remains NULL. Row is immediately reclaimable on next
  relay cycle. VERDICT: reclaimability inherent. No unclaim helper required. I2 pre-check
  PASSES. PRD-082 is NOT blocked on transport-path patch.

write_path_classification: "harness_sql_write_path"
proof_boundary:
  consumer_side_effect_mode: harness_sql_side_effect_required
  requirement: >
    Any proof-state row used as I3/I4 evidence MUST be written inside
    rpc_commit_consumer_receipt in the same PostgreSQL transaction as the
    processed_messages insert. TypeScript-side proof-state writes are forbidden
    for invariant proof.
harness_lifecycle:
  allowed_environments:
    - local_disposable_only
  forbidden_environments:
    - shared_preview
    - shared_staging
    - production
  teardown_requirement: >
    The harness SQL proof-state side effect and outbox_integration_proof_state table
    MUST be removed or explicitly disabled before any Phase 2.1 producer-expansion
    artifact is merged. They are validation-only infrastructure, not standing runtime
    architecture.
runtime_authorization_rule:
  authorized_requires:
    - I1_PASS
    - I2_PASS
    - I3_PASS
    - I4_PASS
    - CASHOUT_NON_EMISSION_PASS
    - DRIFT_CLASSIFICATION_ALL_NON_BLOCKING
  blocking_drift_classes:
    - RELAY_AUTH_BROKEN
    - SERVICE_ROLE_RPC_ACCESS_BROKEN
    - RLS_BOUNDARY_BROKEN
environment_authority:
  signoff_mode: local_disposable_gate
  preview_requirement: >
    Preview comparison is advisory only in this slice unless a follow-on amendment
    explicitly promotes preview drift execution to a blocking gate.
architecture_review:
  outcome: accept_with_conditions
  accepted_option: option_a
  supporting_note: docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/wave-2/w-2-integration-proof/ARCHITECTURE-REVIEW-NOTE-HARNESS-SQL.md
  required_conditions:
    - proof_state_in_real_receipt_transaction
    - local_disposable_execution_only
    - teardown_artifact_required_before_phase_2_1_authorization
    - no_reuse_of_outbox_integration_proof_state_outside_prd_082
    - preview_drift_advisory_only
    - migration_comments_mark_validation_only_infrastructure

workstreams:
  WS1_HARNESS_DDL:
    name: Harness DDL — outbox_integration_proof_state
    description: >
      Migration creating outbox_integration_proof_state table (harness-only; test
      infrastructure for I3/I4 proof capture and replay hashing). Includes a
      harness-only amendment to rpc_commit_consumer_receipt so proof-state capture
      occurs in the same SQL transaction as processed_messages insertion. RLS enabled,
      no policies; service_role access only. GRANT TRUNCATE required for I4 reset phase.
      No application service layer, no DTO, no React Query key.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    traces_to: [O5_replayability, infrastructure]
    outputs:
      - supabase/migrations/20260512000744_add_outbox_integration_proof_state.sql
      - supabase/migrations/20260512000744_add_outbox_integration_proof_state.sql#rpc_commit_consumer_receipt_harness_amendment
      - docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/wave-2/w-2-integration-proof/TEARDOWN-ARTIFACT-PRD-082.md
    gate: schema-validation
    estimated_complexity: low

  WS2_SEED:
    name: Integration Data Seed Procedure
    description: >
      TypeScript seed script creating FK-valid data anchors for both exemplar RPCs.
      Creates Supabase auth user (proof-staff@outbox-proof.local) via auth.admin.createUser
      plus staff record, casino, gaming_table, player, visit, rating_slip with fixed literal
      UUIDs. Idempotent via INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Producer RPCs require authenticated
      session — seed creates and validates the session before exiting.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: []
    traces_to: [O1_real_rpc_execution, seed_real_integration_data]
    outputs:
      - scripts/outbox-proof/seed.ts
    gate: seed-execute
    estimated_complexity: medium

  WS3_PROOF_SCRIPTS:
    name: Invariant Proof Scripts (I1–I4 + runtime drift check)
    description: >
      TypeScript proof scripts using tsx. Authenticated client for producer RPCs;
      service_role client for relay/claim/receipt operations. I1: success + failure
      injection (F14 path for Class A, cross-casino table for Class B); row content
      inspection required. I2: claim without commit → reclaimability proven via
      delivery_attempts = 2 on retry. I3: duplicate event_id → 'duplicate' return +
      single proof-state row written by the receipt SQL transaction. I4: live
      fingerprint vs replay fingerprint comparison (order by event_id; exclude
      consumed_at). Drift check: ENV vars, RPC execution, service_role access,
      CRON_SECRET validation, RLS behavior. Includes explicit reserved-event negative
      test proving cage cashout writes emit no finance_outbox row.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS1_HARNESS_DDL]
    traces_to:
      - O2_atomicity
      - O3_durability
      - O4_idempotency
      - O5_replayability
      - O6_runtime_drift_check
      - invoke_rpc_create_financial_txn
      - invoke_rpc_record_grind_observation
      - drive_relay_path
      - verify_processed_messages_idempotency
      - verify_cashout_non_emission
      - capture_live_proof_state
      - truncate_proof_state
      - replay_from_finance_outbox
      - compare_replay_to_live_state
    outputs:
      - scripts/outbox-proof/helpers.ts
      - scripts/outbox-proof/run-all.ts
      - scripts/outbox-proof/i1-atomicity.ts
      - scripts/outbox-proof/i2-durability.ts
      - scripts/outbox-proof/i3-idempotency.ts
      - scripts/outbox-proof/i4-replayability.ts
      - scripts/outbox-proof/i5-cashout-non-emission.ts
      - scripts/outbox-proof/drift-check.ts
    gate: test-pass
    estimated_complexity: high

  WS4_PROOF_EXECUTE:
    name: Execute Integration Proofs + WAVE-2-INTEGRATION-PROOF-SIGNOFF.md
    description: >
      Executes WS2 seed + WS3 proof suite against local Supabase stack. Preconditions:
      local Supabase running, all migrations applied (including WS1), .env.local populated.
      Commands: supabase db reset --local; npx tsx scripts/outbox-proof/seed.ts;
      npx tsx scripts/outbox-proof/run-all.ts. Populates WAVE-2-INTEGRATION-PROOF-SIGNOFF.md
      with actual I1-I4 results, reserved-event negative-proof result, and runtime drift
      findings. Decision field is binary: "Phase 2.1 authorized" (I1-I4 plus cashout
      non-emission PASS, no blocking drift) or "Phase 2.1 blocked — patch required"
      (any FAIL or blocking drift). If any invariant fails, only transport-path patches
      are permitted; full suite must re-run before issuing authorized decision.
    executor: backend-service-builder
    executor_type: skill
    depends_on: [WS2_SEED, WS3_PROOF_SCRIPTS]
    traces_to:
      - O7_evidence_artifact
      - record_signoff
    outputs:
      - docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/wave-2/w-2-integration-proof/WAVE-2-INTEGRATION-PROOF-SIGNOFF.md
    gate: signoff-validation
    estimated_complexity: low

execution_phases:
  - name: Phase 1 — Harness Infrastructure (parallel)
    parallel: [WS1_HARNESS_DDL, WS2_SEED]
    gates: [schema-validation, seed-execute]

  - name: Phase 2 — Proof Scripts
    parallel: [WS3_PROOF_SCRIPTS]
    gates: [test-pass]

  - name: Phase 3 — Execute and Sign Off
    parallel: [WS4_PROOF_EXECUTE]
    gates: [signoff-validation]

gates:
  schema-validation:
    command: npm run db:types-local
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0; outbox_integration_proof_state visible in generated types"

  seed-execute:
    command: npx tsx scripts/outbox-proof/seed.ts
    success_criteria: "Exit code 0; auth sign-in as proof-staff@outbox-proof.local succeeds"

  test-pass:
    command: bash -lc 'set -o pipefail; npx tsx scripts/outbox-proof/run-all.ts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/outbox-proof-run.log'
    success_criteria: >
      Exit code 0; I1-I4 plus cashout non-emission print PASS in output; proof-state
      writes are attributed to receipt SQL transaction only; no TypeScript-side proof-state
      mutation path exists

  signoff-validation:
    command: >
      bash -lc '
        signoff="docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/wave-2/w-2-integration-proof/WAVE-2-INTEGRATION-PROOF-SIGNOFF.md"
        test -f "$signoff" &&
        for field in environment_tested commands_or_scripts_run I1_result I2_result I3_result I4_result runtime_drift_findings decision; do
          grep -Eq "^${field}: .+" "$signoff" || exit 1
        done &&
        grep -Eq "^decision: (Phase 2.1 authorized|Phase 2.1 blocked — patch required)$" "$signoff" &&
        ! (grep -Eq "^decision: Phase 2.1 authorized$" "$signoff" && grep -Eq "RELAY_AUTH_BROKEN|SERVICE_ROLE_RPC_ACCESS_BROKEN|RLS_BOUNDARY_BROKEN" "$signoff")
      '
    success_criteria: >
      Signoff file exists; all 8 FR-7 fields are present and non-empty; decision field
      contains one of the two authorized values; authorized decision is rejected when
      runtime_drift_findings contains a blocking drift class

external_dependencies:
  - prd: PRD-081
    service: PlayerFinancialService
    required_for: >
      All exemplar infrastructure: finance_outbox DDL, processed_messages DDL,
      rpc_claim_outbox_batch, rpc_commit_consumer_receipt, rpc_create_financial_txn
      (outbox-emitting), rpc_record_grind_observation, relay route. All must be
      present and applied before WS1 migration can run.

decisions:
  - decision_id: DEC-001
    resolves_open_question: "claim_reclaimability_verification"
    decision_statement: >
      rpc_claim_outbox_batch uses WHERE processed_at IS NULL with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED.
      No persistent claim-lock state exists. Reclaimability is inherent in the design.
    rationale: >
      Verified by reading migration 20260511134531. The function updates last_attempted_at
      and delivery_attempts but never sets a claim-lock column. processed_at IS NULL
      persists after claim; row re-enters batch on next relay cycle naturally.
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited

  - decision_id: DEC-002
    resolves_open_question: "outbox_integration_proof_state_migration_scope"
    decision_statement: >
      outbox_integration_proof_state is a harness-only table introduced by this slice.
      It is NOT a projection store, NOT a product read model. Must not be consumed by
      application code outside PRD-082 validation.
    rationale: >
      Explicitly authorized as a harness exception in FIB-S explicit_exclusions and
      PRD-082 §7. Gaming day column omitted (no casino-time semantics). No DTO, no
      service layer, no React Query key.
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited

  - decision_id: DEC-003
    resolves_open_question: "proof_state_atomicity_boundary"
    decision_statement: >
      outbox_integration_proof_state is valid evidence only if rows are written inside
      rpc_commit_consumer_receipt in the same PostgreSQL transaction as processed_messages.
      TypeScript-side proof-state writes do not satisfy PRD-082.
    rationale: >
      The current Wave 2 consumer side effect is intentionally a no-op. Therefore any
      proof that claims atomic duplicate suppression or replay equivalence must anchor its
      evidence to the real SQL receipt boundary, not to post-RPC script behavior.
    impact_on_scope: validation-harness-only
    verification: cited

  - decision_id: DEC-004
    resolves_open_question: "runtime_drift_blocking_policy"
    decision_statement: >
      RELAY_AUTH_BROKEN, SERVICE_ROLE_RPC_ACCESS_BROKEN, and RLS_BOUNDARY_BROKEN are
      blocking drift classes. An authorized signoff is forbidden while any blocking drift
      class is present.
    rationale: >
      The relay route currently rejects requests before DB access when CRON_SECRET is
      missing or mismatched. A runtime gate cannot authorize Phase 2.1 while the real
      relay path is structurally dead or tenant boundaries are broken.
    impact_on_scope: none
    verification: cited

  - decision_id: DEC-005
    resolves_open_question: "harness_sql_architecture_acceptance"
    decision_statement: >
      Architecture accepts Option A for PRD-082: use a harness-only SQL amendment to
      rpc_commit_consumer_receipt, provided proof-state writes remain in the real receipt
      transaction, execution stays local-disposable only, preview drift remains advisory,
      outbox_integration_proof_state is forbidden from reuse outside PRD-082, and an
      explicit teardown artifact exists before any Phase 2.1 authorization.
    rationale: >
      This is the least-bad truthful proof path. Alternative proof designs either prove a
      shadow path or fail to prove the real SQL consumer boundary. Acceptance is
      conditional because the harness must not become standing runtime architecture or a
      reusable projection precedent.
    impact_on_scope: validation-harness-only
    verification: architecture-review

risks:
  - risk: "Producer RPCs require authenticated sessions — service_role alone is insufficient"
    mitigation: >
      WS2 seed creates a Supabase auth user (proof-staff@outbox-proof.local) via
      auth.admin.createUser(). WS3 helpers.ts exports signInAsStaff() for proof scripts.
  - risk: "Relay claim lock prevents reclaimability on interruption"
    mitigation: >
      DEC-001 resolves this: reclaimability is inherent. No mitigation needed.
      delivery_attempts = 2 is the I2 evidence.
  - risk: "CRON_SECRET not set locally"
    mitigation: >
      Drift check includes ENV_VARS and CRON_SECRET_VALIDATION checks. Missing or invalid
      CRON_SECRET is classified RELAY_AUTH_BROKEN. Local direct RPC proofs may still run,
      but Phase 2.1 authorization is blocked until relay auth is functional.
  - risk: "Replay hash non-deterministic due to timestamp ordering"
    mitigation: >
      Fingerprint sorts by event_id (UUIDv7 = monotonic insertion order), not by
      consumed_at (wall-clock time). consumed_at excluded from hash computation.
  - risk: "I4 replay blocked by processed_messages idempotency guard"
    mitigation: >
      Replay procedure TRUNCATEs processed_messages between live and replay runs.
      Environment is expendable synthetic data only (NFR-1).
---

# EXECUTION-SPEC: PRD-082 — Wave 2 Integration Proof

## Overview

PRD-081 delivered the transactional outbox exemplar structurally. This EXEC-SPEC is the
hardening gate that proves the exemplar transport chain survives real runtime execution
before any Phase 2.1 producer expansion begins. It is a validation-and-test-harness-only
slice — no new business behavior, no producer wiring, no projection stores, no operator
surfaces. The work produces one migration (harness table), a seed script, runnable proof
scripts for four invariants (I1 Atomicity, I2 Durability, I3 Idempotency, I4 Replayability),
one reserved-event negative proof (`cashout.recorded` non-emission), and a signed-off
evidence artifact whose decision field gates Phase 2.1.

## Scope

- **In Scope**: `outbox_integration_proof_state` harness table migration; seed procedure
  for integration data anchors; I1–I4 proof scripts; runtime drift check; signoff artifact.
- **Out of Scope**: Producer expansion; projection stores; dashboard; operator UI; ADR
  amendments (052–055); broad schema redesign. See FIB-S explicit_exclusions.

## Containment Rules

- Harness SQL side effects are permitted only for this validation gate and only in disposable local environments.
- `outbox_integration_proof_state` is evidence infrastructure, not a standing read model, projection, or reusable consumer substrate.
- The harness receipt amendment must be removed or disabled before Phase 2.1 producer-expansion work merges.
- Shared preview/staging and production are forbidden execution targets for any flow that truncates `processed_messages` or proof-state.

## Architecture Decision

Architecture review accepts **Option A** for this EXEC-SPEC with conditions:

- Keep proof-state writes inside the real `rpc_commit_consumer_receipt` transaction.
- Restrict execution to disposable local environments only.
- Require an explicit teardown artifact now, before any Phase 2.1 authorization.
- Forbid `outbox_integration_proof_state` reuse outside PRD-082.
- Keep preview drift advisory for this slice.
- Require migration comments to classify the harness as validation-only infrastructure.

The supporting review note remains useful context, but execution authority lives here in
`EXEC-082`.

## Architecture Context

All exemplar infrastructure is from PRD-081 (migrations 20260511134015–20260511135047,
relay route `app/api/internal/outbox-relay/route.ts`). This slice adds only one migration.
Bounded context: financial-outbox (PlayerFinancialService). No new bounded context.

Key finding (DEC-001): `rpc_claim_outbox_batch` has no persistent claim-lock state.
Reclaimability is inherent via `processed_at IS NULL` filter. I2 pre-check PASSES.

Key boundary (DEC-003): I3/I4 proof-state evidence is valid only when written inside
`rpc_commit_consumer_receipt` in the same SQL transaction as `processed_messages`.
TypeScript-side proof-state writes are not admissible evidence for this gate.

## Workstream Details

### WS1_HARNESS_DDL: Harness DDL

**Purpose**: Create `outbox_integration_proof_state` table for I3/I4 proof-state capture and
amend the receipt boundary so proof-state writes occur inside SQL, not in TypeScript. This is a
validation-only transport shim with an explicit teardown requirement.

**Deliverables**:
1. `supabase/migrations/20260512000744_add_outbox_integration_proof_state.sql`
2. Same migration includes the harness-only `rpc_commit_consumer_receipt` amendment
3. `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/wave-2/w-2-integration-proof/TEARDOWN-ARTIFACT-PRD-082.md`

**Schema** (key columns):
- `seq BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY` — monotonic order for deterministic comparison
- `event_id UUID NOT NULL` — mirrors `finance_outbox.event_id`
- `event_type`, `fact_class`, `origin_label`, `casino_id`, `table_id`, `player_id`, `aggregate_id`, `payload`
- `consumed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()` — excluded from I4 fingerprint (wall-clock diverges)

**Boundary rule**:
- `outbox_integration_proof_state` rows are inserted only by harness SQL inside
  `rpc_commit_consumer_receipt`
- Proof scripts may read and truncate proof-state; they must not insert proof-state rows
- One logical consumer commit may create at most one proof-state row per `event_id`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `npm run db:types-local` exits 0
- [ ] RLS enabled; no policies; `GRANT TRUNCATE TO service_role`
- [ ] Table truncatable via service_role
- [ ] `consumed_at` excluded from fingerprint query
- [ ] Harness receipt SQL writes proof-state in the same transaction as `processed_messages`
- [ ] Unique proof-state duplication guard exists for `event_id`
- [ ] Migration comment and rollback notes mark the harness amendment as validation-only
- [ ] Teardown artifact exists and defines how the harness receipt amendment and proof-state table are removed or disabled before Phase 2.1 authorization

### WS2_SEED: Integration Data Seed Procedure

**Purpose**: Create FK-valid data anchors with fixed UUIDs for deterministic proof execution.

**Deliverables**:
1. `scripts/outbox-proof/seed.ts`

**Fixed UUIDs** (referenced by all proof scripts):
- Casino: `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001`
- Gaming table: `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002`
- Player: `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003`
- Visit: `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000004`
- Rating slip: `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000005`
- Staff auth: `proof-staff@outbox-proof.local` / `ProofHarness2026!`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Script exits 0; idempotent on re-run
- [ ] Auth sign-in as proof-staff succeeds (valid JWT for producer RPC calls)
- [ ] FK chain intact: `rating_slip.table_id` → `gaming_table.id` → `casino.id`
- [ ] Seed creates two-casino fixtures sufficient for cross-casino negative proofs

### WS3_PROOF_SCRIPTS: Invariant Proof Scripts

**Purpose**: Runnable TypeScript scripts exercising all four invariants and drift checklist.

**Deliverables**:
1. `scripts/outbox-proof/helpers.ts` — shared utilities
2. `scripts/outbox-proof/run-all.ts` — orchestrator
3. `scripts/outbox-proof/i1-atomicity.ts` — I1 success + failure injection for both producers
4. `scripts/outbox-proof/i2-durability.ts` — claim without commit → reclaimability → retry
5. `scripts/outbox-proof/i3-idempotency.ts` — duplicate delivery → 'duplicate' + one proof-state row
6. `scripts/outbox-proof/i4-replayability.ts` — live fingerprint vs replay fingerprint
7. `scripts/outbox-proof/i5-cashout-non-emission.ts` — reserved cashout negative proof
8. `scripts/outbox-proof/drift-check.ts` — ENV, RPC, relay, RLS, CRON_SECRET checks

**I1 failure injection**: Use F14 path (nonexistent `p_rating_slip_id` for Class A;
cross-casino `p_table_id` for Class B) — exception fires AFTER first INSERT, proving
both writes roll back atomically.

**I3 boundary**: duplicate suppression proof is valid only if proof-state is inserted by
`rpc_commit_consumer_receipt` SQL. Any proof-state insertion outside that transaction is a FAIL.

**I4 fingerprint**: `md5(string_agg(... ORDER BY event_id))` — excludes `consumed_at`;
`processed_messages` TRUNCATED between live and replay to allow re-processing.

**Reserved-event negative proof**: Cage cashout (`direction='out'`, current Wave 2 path) must
persist the financial write when otherwise valid but emit no `finance_outbox` row.

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `npx tsx scripts/outbox-proof/run-all.ts` exits 0 against local stack
- [ ] All invariants return `{ pass: true }`; row content inspection performed (not count-only)
- [ ] No mock substitution in any proof path
- [ ] I3 proves one proof-state row created by receipt SQL transaction, not by TypeScript
- [ ] Explicit cage cashout negative test proves zero `finance_outbox` rows emitted
- [ ] Cross-casino Class B proof uses a second casino fixture and asserts zero telemetry + zero outbox rows

### WS4_PROOF_EXECUTE: Execute + Signoff

**Purpose**: Run the proof suite and write the signed-off evidence artifact.

**Environment authority**: This slice authorizes a disposable local-runtime gate only.
Preview drift comparison may be recorded, but it is non-blocking unless a later amendment says otherwise.

**Execution commands** (ordered):
```bash
npx supabase db reset --local                          # clean state + all migrations
npx tsx scripts/outbox-proof/seed.ts                   # seed anchors
bash -lc 'set -o pipefail; npx tsx scripts/outbox-proof/run-all.ts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/outbox-proof-run.log'
```

**Decision rule**: `decision: Phase 2.1 authorized` IFF all of I1, I2, I3, I4, and
cashout non-emission = PASS AND all runtime drift findings are classified non-blocking.
Blocking classes: `RELAY_AUTH_BROKEN`, `SERVICE_ROLE_RPC_ACCESS_BROKEN`,
`RLS_BOUNDARY_BROKEN`. Any FAIL or blocking drift → `decision: Phase 2.1 blocked — patch required`
(transport-path patch only; full suite re-run before authorized decision).

**Signoff path**: `docs/issues/gaps/financial-data-distribution-standard/wave-2/w-2-integration-proof/WAVE-2-INTEGRATION-PROOF-SIGNOFF.md`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Signoff file exists at designated path
- [ ] All 8 required fields present and non-empty (FR-7)
- [ ] Decision field is one of the two authorized binary values
- [ ] Authorized decision is absent when any blocking drift class is recorded
- [ ] `environment_tested` declares a disposable local environment, not shared preview/staging or production

## Definition of Done

- [ ] I1 Atomicity proven for both `rpc_create_financial_txn` and `rpc_record_grind_observation` (content inspection)
- [ ] I2 Durability proven: `delivery_attempts = 2` evidence on retry; `processed_at` set after retry
- [ ] I3 Idempotency proven: second delivery returns `'duplicate'`; single proof-state row written by receipt SQL transaction
- [ ] I4 Replayability proven: `live_fingerprint === replay_fingerprint`
- [ ] Reserved `cashout.recorded` non-emission proven for current Wave 2 cage cashout path
- [ ] Runtime drift check executed and findings recorded
- [ ] `WAVE-2-INTEGRATION-PROOF-SIGNOFF.md` exists, all fields non-empty, decision set
- [ ] Harness receipt amendment and proof-state table have an explicit teardown/disable follow-up before Phase 2.1 merge
- [ ] `TEARDOWN-ARTIFACT-PRD-082.md` exists before any Phase 2.1 authorization is issued
- [ ] No producer expansion, projection stores, UI changes, or ADR amendments introduced
- [ ] `npm run db:types-local` exits 0 after WS1 migration
